data.title: U.S. New Home Sales Rise in November But Fall in December, Delayed Data Say
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes

The rate of new home sales jumped in November, but fell back slightly in December. Here are the main takeaways from the delayed report released by the Census Bureau and the Department of Housing and Urban Development Friday:

  • Sales of new single-family homes rose to 758,000 in November, from 656,000 in October, and slipped to 745,000 in December.
  • A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected 738,000 sales in December.
  • The estimate for new houses for sale was 472,000 in December, compared with 485,000 in November, the Commerce Department said.
  • The median sales price of new houses sold in December was $414,400, up 4.2% from November, it added.
  • The combined November and December reports were originally scheduled for publication on Dec. 23 and Jan. 27, but were delayed due to the federal government shutdown.

Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com

data.title: NOV Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >NOV
data.description:
NOV Inc. (NOV) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 20, 2026.

Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Appointment of Officers

On February 19, 2026, Ben A. Guill, director of NOV Inc. (the "Company"), informed the Company's Board of Directors (the "Board") that he resigned as a member of the Board effective following the Company's Board meeting on February 19, 2026 due to his future personal schedule and other time commitments. Mr. Guill's resignation from the Board is not the result of any dispute or disagreement with the Company or the Board on any matter relating to the Company's operations, policies, or practices.

The Company thanks Mr. Guill for his distinguished service on the Board and significant contributions to the Company.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1021860/000119312526060618/nov-20260219.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1021860/000119312526060618/0001193125-26-060618-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

Cum Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, including income, with debt at par value.

Cum Fair NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, including income, with debt at fair value.

Ex Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, excluding income, with debt at par value.

Ex Fair NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, excluding income, with debt at fair value.

data.title: EU Taking Stock Following Supreme Court Tariff Ruling, Spokesperson Says
data.description:
By Edith Hancock

The European Union is carefully analyzing the Supreme Court's ruling that U.S. President Trump's tariffs are illegal, a spokesperson for the European Commission said.

"We remain in close contact with the U.S. Administration as we seek clarity on the steps they intend to take in response to this ruling," spokesperson Olof Gill said.

"Businesses on both sides of the Atlantic depend on stability and predictability in the trading relationship. "We therefore continue to advocate for low tariffs and to work towards reducing them."

Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: US stocks gain as court rules against Trump's tariffs
data.description:
Key points:
  • US stocks higher after tariff ruling
  • Comm Svcs leads S&P 500 sector gainers; Energy weakest group
  • Dollar, crude fall; bitcoin, gold gain
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield flat at ~4.08%

US STOCKS GAIN AS COURT RULES AGAINST TRUMP'S TARIFFS

U.S. stocks are rising on Friday after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled against President Donald Trump's tariffs imposed on imports under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

"On the one hand, I think investors have for a while felt like the tariffs were a negative, so the market's reaction to go up initially is a reaction of relief that maybe this brings an end to tariffs as the main strategy the administration was using," said Rick Meckler, partner, Cherry Lane Investments, a family investment office in New Vernon, New Jersey.

"The negative of it is what happens with the money that's already been paid. And the fact that this administration has counted on tariffs as a budget balancing technique is going to create some chaos."

Reuters had reported that Penn Wharton Budget Model economists said that if the tariffs were struck down, there was a risk that more than $175 billion in U.S. tariff collections may need to be refunded.

Here is the early market snapshot:

data.title: Stocks Climb After Supreme Court Strikes Down Tariffs — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Stocks mostly gained Friday, after the Supreme Court struck down President Trump's sweeping tariffs, finding that he exceeded his powers by imposing duties without clear Congressional authorization.

The six-three decision by the court fueled a rally in transportation stocks, and in tariff-exposed companies such as Nike and Abercrombie & Fitch. The dollar slipped and Treasury yields edged higher.

The economy slowed more than expected into the end of the year, to a 1.4% rate, as the record government shutdown prompted a steep drop in federal spending. Separate data showed inflation, as measured by the Federal Reserve's preferred PCE gauge, running at 2.9% - a touch higher than expected.

Oil prices slipped after the WSJ reported Trump is weighing a targeted strike on military or government sites in Iran to pressure Tehran into a deal, rather than a full-scale attack. Brent crude oil futures are still up more than 5% since last Friday, on pace for their biggest weekly gain since October.

Gold and silver futures rose. In global stock markets, indexes in Hong Kong and Japan retreated just over 1%. European stocks advanced.

Write to Caitlin McCabe at caitlin.mccabe@wsj.com

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: European Auto Stocks Gain After Supreme Court Strikes Down Trump's Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: Shares in European automotive manufacturers and suppliers edge higher after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled President Trump's global tariffs are illegal. Shares of Jeep maker Stellantis are up 2.7% in Milan. In Germany, Volkswagen shares are up 0.8%, while Mercedes-Benz Group stock is up 0.7%. In France, Renault shares are up 0.9%. Shares of tiremaker Pirelli are up 0.3%. The sector had been one of the first casualties of tariffs early last year before a deal between the U.S. and the European Union restored some predictability. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

data.title: Court Ruling Does Not Mark Reset of US Trade Policy, Canadian Chamber Warns — Market Talk
data.description: Canada's largest business group isn't popping champagne corks about the Supreme Court's decision to strike down President Trump's global tariffs. This "is a legal ruling, not a reset of U.S. trade policy," says Candace Laing, president of the Canadian Chamber of Commerce. "Canada should prepare for new, blunter mechanisms to be used to reassert trade pressure, potentially with broader and more disruptive effects." Most Canadian exports were largely exempt from the IEEPA tariffs because Canadian exports complied with USMCA. That pact is up for review this year, with widespread expectations that Washington will demand more concessions from Ottawa to keep preferential trade access intact.(Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Superior Plus Shares Fall on 4Q Revenue Miss, Delayed Transformation Timeline
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Superior Plus shares fell sharply Friday morning after the company reported lower fourth-quarter revenue and said it would take longer to complete its transformation.

Shares trading in Toronto fell 18% to 6.58 Canadian dollars ($4.81).

The Toronto-based distributor of propane, compressed natural gas, and renewable energy late Thursday reported revenue that fell to $691 million from $702.3 million, missing forecasts of a slight rise to $704.7 million, according to FactSet.

Net earnings rose to $49.1 million from $4.2 million, which on a per-share basis was 18 cents, up from zero.

Adjusted earnings were 27 cents a share, above the 19 cents a share expected by Wall Street.

In 2024, the company launched its Superior Delivers transformation program to improve long-term growth by boosting efficiency, profitability and balance-sheet strength.

Chief Executive Allan MacDonald said the company made operational gains in 2025, modernizing parts of its network and improving productivity, but said the rollout of those changes created service strains in some regions. He said the pressures were made worse by cold weather and higher demand, forcing the company to push back its transformation plans.

"We have extended the timeline needed to complete the full transformation," MacDonald said.

The delay prompted a downgrade to the stock by TD Cowen, taking it from a buy rating to hold and lowering the price target to C$7 from C$8.

Analyst Aaron MacNeil said in a report: "With three consecutive quarters featuring material negative surprises, as well as two consecutive years of annual guidance misses, we believe that Superior Plus is in 'show me' territory."

"We are moving to hold until we see a multi-quarter trend of meeting expectations," the analyst added.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Oil States International Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Oil States International Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 13 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 9 cents. The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 10 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 10 cents to 11 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 8.4% to $178.46 million from a year ago; analysts expected $180.16 million.

  • Oil States International Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $2.04​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $177.25 million.

  • Oil States International Inc shares had risen by 47.7% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the oil related services and equipment peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Oil States International Inc is $8.50, about 17.6% below its last closing price of $10.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 20 at 03:31 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: SEM: Q4 revenue rose 6% year-over-year, but margins fell on higher health costs and outpatient softness
data.description:
Q4 revenue grew over 6% year-over-year, but Adjusted EBITDA declined 10% due to higher health insurance costs. Inpatient rehab led growth, while outpatient margins fell on payer mix and discounts. 2026 guidance projects revenue of $5.6B–$5.8B and margin improvement in outpatient.

Based on

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: US-Iran Tensions: Trump Reportedly Says He Is Considering A Limited Military Strike On The Middle-Eastern Country
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Friday reportedly said that he is considering a limited military strike on Iran.

“I guess I can say I am considering (it),” President Trump told reporters at the White House when asked if he was evaluating a limited strike to pressure Iran to sign a nuclear deal with the U.S., according to a Reuters report.

This comes a day after the President gave Iran 10 to 15 days to strike a nuclear deal.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.
